selections.name: Whiskey Shot
selections.reason: Drops from a tougher $16k claiming event at Churchill Downs. The Churchill spillover angle is strong here, and his late pace figures fit perfectly with the long Ellis Park stretch.

selections.name: Timberline
selections.reason: Taking a significant class drop from $20k non-winners of two at Churchill. Has the back class and tactical speed to sit a good trip.

selections.name: Romantic Lead
selections.reason: Drawn the rail and possesses a closing style that will benefit from the 1,175-foot stretch. Forgiving the last start where he was wide.

selections.name: Jinxzi
selections.reason: Drops in class and gets a favorable outside draw to stalk the pace. Has shown flashes of ability against better.

danger_horse.name: American Bear
danger_horse.reason: Could get brave if left alone on the lead, though the long stretch makes him vulnerable late.

selections.name: Mitty's Griddy
selections.reason: Boasts the highest speed and class figures in the field. Drops from a tough $20k claiming race at Churchill and has the tactical speed to pounce.

selections.name: Ship Ship Hooray
selections.reason: Broke his maiden impressively over this track last summer. Has the class to compete here and gets a good outside draw.

selections.name: Gallo De Fuego
selections.reason: Ran a strong second in a $20k claimer at Churchill last out. Fits the class profile and should be rolling late.

selections.name: Guardian
selections.reason: Won a $30k claimer at Churchill recently. The rail draw is tricky, but he has the form to hit the board.

danger_horse.name: Drewvictory
danger_horse.reason: Has back class and speed figures that make him a threat if he can recapture his best form.

selections.name: Lucius Verus
selections.reason: Taking a massive class drop from allowance company into a $16k claimer. The outside post is ideal for a stalking trip.

selections.name: Historic
selections.reason: Consistent runner with solid speed figures. The rail draw means he will have to be used early, but he fits on class.

selections.name: Army Wildcatter
selections.reason: Has faced tougher fields in Louisiana and Kentucky. Should appreciate the class relief today.

selections.name: Jack Charles (gb)
selections.reason: Broke his maiden in the slop at Churchill. Needs to prove it on a fast track but has upside.

danger_horse.name: Colonel Compton
danger_horse.reason: Has tactical speed and could pick up the pieces if the pace melts down.

selections.name: Bardstown Road
selections.reason: Ran a strong third in a Churchill Downs MSW last out. The Churchill spillover angle is potent here, and he has the best turf pedigree.

selections.name: Play It Again
selections.reason: Finished fifth in a tough MSW at Churchill. The outside draw is a slight negative, but he has the tactical speed to overcome it.

selections.name: Agate
selections.reason: Showed some late interest in his debut. Should improve second time out and gets a favorable inside post.

selections.name: Sky Warrior
selections.reason: Has room to improve after a learning experience on debut. Mark Casse barn is always dangerous with second-time starters.

danger_horse.name: Castiglione
danger_horse.reason: First-time starter for Cherie DeVaux with Brian Hernandez aboard. Workouts suggest readiness.

selections.name: French Blue
selections.reason: Bob Baffert shipper with massive class edge. Placed in the G2 SA Oaks and G3 Santa Ysabel. Should dominate this allowance field.

selections.name: Rational Theory
selections.reason: Consistent Asmussen runner with strong Churchill form. Will get a great ground-saving trip from the rail.

selections.name: If If If
selections.reason: Broke her maiden impressively at Oaklawn. Has the speed figures to contend if she can transfer that form to Ellis.

selections.name: Overcome Adversity
selections.reason: Has back class and solid speed figures. Should be closing late if the pace is hot.

danger_horse.name: Vicki Rose
danger_horse.reason: Veteran mare who always tries hard, but might be outclassed by the top contenders.

selections.name: Tirupati
selections.reason: Brings graded stakes form from California. Jonathan Thomas and Umberto Rispoli are a lethal combination. Huge class advantage.

selections.name: Queens Command (ire)
selections.reason: Has been facing tough graded stakes company. The drop into an AOC is exactly what she needs to find the winner's circle.

selections.name: Daisy Flyer
selections.reason: Consistent turf router for Rusty Arnold. Will be flying late and should appreciate the firm going.

selections.name: Temptable (gb)
selections.reason: Bill Mott trainee with European form. Has the tactical speed to sit a perfect trip from an inside post.

danger_horse.name: Bourbon Memory
danger_horse.reason: Has back class and could surprise at a price if she gets a clean trip from the outside.

selections.name: Embry Show
selections.reason: Bob Baffert trainee who ran a strong third in a Churchill MSW on debut. Should step forward massively second time out.

selections.name: Pursuit Of Freedom
selections.reason: Shipper from Santa Anita for Peter Eurton. Has the speed figures to contend and gets class relief from the West Coast.

selections.name: Lifes Reward
selections.reason: Ran second in a Churchill claimer last out. The rail draw is tough, but he has experience and tactical speed.

selections.name: Top Drawer
selections.reason: First-time starter for Brad Cox. Workouts are solid, and this barn strikes at a high percentage with debut runners.

danger_horse.name: Native Brew
danger_horse.reason: Asmussen first-timer who could show speed and hang around for a minor award.

selections.name: Beach Heist
selections.reason: Undefeated turf sprinter who won an allowance at Churchill Downs last out. Has the rail and the speed to wire this field.

selections.name: Triskelion
selections.reason: Ran a strong second to a nice horse at Churchill. The outside draw is a slight concern, but she has the class to overcome it.

selections.name: Sutura
selections.reason: Finished fourth in the Penn Oaks. Cutting back in distance could be exactly what she needs to show her best turn of foot.

selections.name: Just A Minute
selections.reason: Has tactical speed and solid form. Should get a good stalking trip from the outside post.

danger_horse.name: Woodstock
danger_horse.reason: Has back class and could pick up the pieces if the pace is suicidal.

selections.name: Infinite Sky
selections.reason: Massive class edge. Finished 6th in the Breeders' Cup Juvenile Fillies Turf and 2nd in the G2 Jessamine. This is a huge drop in class.

selections.name: Embrace Time
selections.reason: Brad Cox trainee who ran a solid fourth in a Churchill allowance. Has the pedigree and connections to be a major factor.

selections.name: Trust Account
selections.reason: Faced tough company in the G3 Regret. The class relief here makes her a prime contender for the exotics.

selections.name: Rhythm Lady
selections.reason: Has tactical speed and the rail draw. Should save all the ground and be in a position to strike turning for home.

danger_horse.name: Market Chill
danger_horse.reason: Chad Brown trainee who is always dangerous, though she needs to prove she can handle this level of competition.
